curriculum.subject: Accountancy
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 9 chapters, and 6 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Describe the meaning, significance, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting in the modern economic environment with varied types of business and non-business economic entities.
Identify users of accounting information and their needs regarding qualitative characteristics of accounting information.

Explain fundamental accounting assumptions (GAAP) including Business Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, Cost Concept, Dual Aspect, Revenue Recognition, Matching, Full Disclosure, Consistency, Conservatism, Materiality, and Objectivity.
Analyze the effect of business transactions on assets, liabilities, capital, revenue, and expenses using the accounting equation approach.
Prepare source documents and vouchers for various trade discounts, freight, cartage, and GST scenarios.
Record business transactions in Books of Original Entry including Journal (with special entries), Cash Book (Simple, Bank column, Petty cashbook) and Special Purpose Books.
Understand the core concepts of Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship.
Apply key ideas from Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship to solve curriculum-aligned problems.
Analyze relationships between major concepts in Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship.
Explain the concept, objectives, and limitations of accounting along with its role in business.
Record accounting transactions accurately using the double entry system including journals, cash books, ledgers, and depreciation calculations.
Interpret financial data through ratio analysis and prepare a statement of cash flows.

topics: Basic Accounting Terms: Entity, Capital, Drawings, Liabilities, Assets, Expenditure, Expense, Revenue, Income, Profit, Gain, Loss, Purchase, Sales, Goods, Stock, Debtor, Creditor, Voucher, Discount

Fundamental Accounting Assumptions and Concepts: Business Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, Accounting Period, Cost Concept, Dual Aspect, Revenue Recognition, Matching, Full Disclosure, Consistency, Conservatism, Materiality, Objectivity
System of Accounting: Cash Basis vs Accrual Basis
Accounting Standards (AS) and Indian Accounting Standards (IndAS)
Goods and Services Tax (GST): Characteristics and Advantages
Source Documents and Vouchers: Preparation, Accounting Equation Approach, Rules of Debit and Credit
Books of Original Entry: Journal (including trade discount, freight, cartage for GST), Cash Book (Simple, with bank column, Petty cashbook)
Special Purpose Books: Purchases book, Sales book, Purchases return book, Sales return book, Journal proper

units.unitName: Unit-3: Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship

topics: Introduction to Accounting and Theoretical Framework
Qualitative Characteristics and Role in Business
Basic Accounting Terms: Assets, Liabilities, Capital, Revenue vs Expenditure
Fundamental Assumptions (GAAP): Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, etc.
Basis of Accounting: Cash vs Accrual
Source Documents, Vouchers, and Journal Entries
Special Purpose Books: Cash Book (Simple & Bank), Petty Cash Book
Ledger Posting and Balancing of Accounts
Depreciation Methods (SLM vs WDV) and Treatment

subtopics: Basic Accounting Terms: Entity, Capital, Drawings, Liabilities, Assets, Expenditure, Expense, Revenue, Income, Profit, Gain, Loss, Purchase, Sales, Goods, Stock, Debtor, Creditor, Voucher, Discount

Fundamental Accounting Assumptions and Concepts: Business Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, Accounting Period, Cost Concept, Dual Aspect, Revenue Recognition, Matching, Full Disclosure, Consistency, Conservatism, Materiality, Objectivity
System of Accounting: Cash Basis vs Accrual Basis
Accounting Standards (AS) and Indian Accounting Standards (IndAS)
Goods and Services Tax (GST): Characteristics and Advantages
Source Documents and Vouchers: Preparation, Accounting Equation Approach, Rules of Debit and Credit
Books of Original Entry: Journal (including trade discount, freight, cartage for GST), Cash Book (Simple, with bank column, Petty cashbook)
Special Purpose Books: Purchases book, Sales book, Purchases return book, Sales return book, Journal proper
key_concepts: Posting from Subsidiary Books to Ledger

units.unit_name: Unit-3: Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship

topics: Introduction to Accounting and Theoretical Framework
subtopics: Concept, Objectives, Limitations of Accounting
Qualitative Characteristics and Role in Business
Basic Accounting Terms: Assets, Liabilities, Capital, Revenue vs Expenditure
Fundamental Assumptions (GAAP): Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, etc.
Basis of Accounting: Cash vs Accrual
Source Documents, Vouchers, and Journal Entries
Special Purpose Books: Cash Book (Simple & Bank), Petty Cash Book
Purchases/Sales Books and Returns
Ledger Posting and Balancing of Accounts
Depreciation Methods (SLM vs WDV) and Treatment

practicals.title: Practical Work: Computerised Accounting System
practicals.details: Steps in installation of CAS, codification and Hierarchy of account heads, creation of accounts; Data Entry, validation and verification; Adjusting entries, preparation of balance sheet, profit and loss account with closing entries and opening entries.

projects.title: Project Work: Collection of Source Documents and Recording
projects.details: Collection of source documents, preparation of vouchers, recording of transactions with the help of vouchers.

projects.title: Project Work: Bank Reconciliation Statement
projects.details: Preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement with the given cash book and the pass book with twenty to twenty-five transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Comprehensive Sole Proprietorship Business
projects.details: Comprehensive project of any sole proprietorship business. This may start with journal entries and their ledgering, preparation of Trial balance, Trading and Profit and Loss Account and Balance Sheet. Expenses, incomes and profit (loss), assets and liabilities are to be depicted using pie chart / bar diagram. May include simple GST related transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Financial Statement Analysis
projects.details: One specific project based on financial statement analysis of a company covering any two aspects from the following: Comparative and common size financial statements, Accounting Ratios, Segment Reports, Cash Flow Statements.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (80 Marks), Project Work (20 Marks)
Class XII Part A & B Combined: Theory (60 Marks for Partnership/Companies, 12 Marks for Analysis, 8 Marks for Cash Flow) + Project/Viva (20 Marks)
question_paper_design: Class XI Class XII: Remembering and Understanding - 32 Marks (40%), Applying - 24 Marks (30%), Analysing/Evaluating/Creating - 24 Marks (30%)
bloom_levels: 1. Remembering and Understanding (Recall facts, terms, concepts; organize, compare, interpret)
2. Applying (Solve problems to new situations using acquired knowledge)
3. Analysing, Evaluating and Creating (Examine parts, make inferences, defend opinions, compile information)
internal_assessment: Class XII Part A/B Option 1: Project File (12 Marks) + Viva Voce (8 Marks)
Class XII Part B Option 2: Practical File (12 Marks) + Viva Voce (8 Marks)
practical_assessment: Collection of source documents and preparation of vouchers
Recording transactions with help of vouchers
Preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement (20-25 transactions)
Comprehensive project: Journal entries, Ledgering, Trial Balance, Trading & P&L Account, Balance Sheet
project_assessment: Class XI: Comprehensive sole proprietorship business project including GST transactions and graphical depiction of expenses/incomes
Class XII Part A/B Option 1: Specific project on financial statement analysis (Comparative/Common size statements, Accounting Ratios, Segment Reports, or Cash Flow Statements)
Class XII Part B Option 2: Computerised Accounting System practical work

competency_groups.title: Theoretical Framework Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from Unit-1: Theoretical Framework covering accounting concepts, terms, and assumptions.
competencies: Describe the meaning, significance, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting in the modern economic environment with varied types of business and non-business economic entities.
Identify users of accounting information and their needs regarding qualitative characteristics of accounting information.

Explain fundamental accounting assumptions (GAAP) including Business Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, Cost Concept, Dual Aspect, Revenue Recognition, Matching, Full Disclosure, Consistency, Conservatism, Materiality, and Objectivity.

competency_groups.title: Accounting Process Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from Unit-2: Accounting Process covering transaction recording and bookkeeping.
competencies: Analyze the effect of business transactions on assets, liabilities, capital, revenue, and expenses using the accounting equation approach.
Prepare source documents and vouchers for various trade discounts, freight, cartage, and GST scenarios.
Record business transactions in Books of Original Entry including Journal (with special entries), Cash Book (Simple, Bank column, Petty cashbook) and Special Purpose Books.

competency_groups.title: Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship.
competencies: Understand the core concepts of Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship.
Apply key ideas from Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship to solve curriculum-aligned problems.
Analyze relationships between major concepts in Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-3: Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship

competency_groups.title: Foundations of Accounting and Theoretical Framework
competency_groups.description: Demonstrate understanding of accounting concepts, objectives, limitations, qualitative characteristics, fundamental assumptions (GAAP), basis of accounting, and GST applicability.
competencies: Explain the concept, objectives, and limitations of accounting along with its role in business.

competency_groups.title: Accounting Process and Transaction Recording
competency_groups.description: Apply rules of debit and credit to record transactions using source documents, vouchers, journal entries, special purpose books, ledger posting, depreciation methods, trial balance verification, rectification of errors, bank reconciliation, and distinction between capital/revenue items.
competencies: Record accounting transactions accurately using the double entry system including journals, cash books, ledgers, and depreciation calculations.

competency_groups.title: Analysis of Financial Statements and Cash Flow
competency_groups.description: Analyze financial statements to assess performance, liquidity, solvency, and efficiency; prepare cash flow statements classifying activities into operating, investing, and financing.
competencies: Interpret financial data through ratio analysis and prepare a statement of cash flows.

competency_groups.title: Computerised Accounting Systems
competency_groups.description: Utilize computerized accounting software for data entry, processing transactions, generating reports, and maintaining digital records.
competencies: Operate basic functions of accounting software to record transactions and generate financial statements.

competency_groups.title: Cash Flow Statement Preparation and Analysis
competency_groups.description: Prepare cash flow statements using direct or indirect methods, classify cash flows into operating, investing, and financing activities, and analyze liquidity implications.
competencies: Construct a Cash Flow Statement by classifying inflows and outflows from operating, investing, and financing activities.

competency_groups.title: Fundamentals of Computerized Accounting Systems
competency_groups.description: Understanding the basic principles, advantages, and components involved in transitioning from manual to computerized accounting processes.
competencies: Identify key differences between manual and computerized accounting systems regarding efficiency and accuracy.
Explain the role of hardware and software requirements for implementing a successful computerized accounting solution.
Analyze the impact of automation on data entry, processing speed, and error reduction in financial record-keeping.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (80 Marks), Project Work (20 Marks)
Class XII Part A & B Combined: Theory (60 Marks for Partnership/Companies, 12 Marks for Analysis, 8 Marks for Cash Flow) + Project/Viva (20 Marks)
question_paper_design: Class XI Class XII: Remembering and Understanding - 32 Marks (40%), Applying - 24 Marks (30%), Analysing/Evaluating/Creating - 24 Marks (30%)
bloom_levels: 1. Remembering and Understanding (Recall facts, terms, concepts; organize, compare, interpret)
2. Applying (Solve problems to new situations using acquired knowledge)
3. Analysing, Evaluating and Creating (Examine parts, make inferences, defend opinions, compile information)
internal_assessment: Class XII Part A/B Option 1: Project File (12 Marks) + Viva Voce (8 Marks)
Class XII Part B Option 2: Practical File (12 Marks) + Viva Voce (8 Marks)
practical_assessment: Collection of source documents and preparation of vouchers
Recording transactions with help of vouchers
Preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement (20-25 transactions)
Comprehensive project: Journal entries, Ledgering, Trial Balance, Trading & P&L Account, Balance Sheet
project_assessment: Class XI: Comprehensive sole proprietorship business project including GST transactions and graphical depiction of expenses/incomes
Class XII Part A/B Option 1: Specific project on financial statement analysis (Comparative/Common size statements, Accounting Ratios, Segment Reports, or Cash Flow Statements)
Class XII Part B Option 2: Computerised Accounting System practical work

outcomes: Describe the meaning, significance, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting in the modern economic environment with varied types of business and non-business economic entities.
Identify users of accounting information and their needs regarding qualitative characteristics of accounting information.

Explain fundamental accounting assumptions (GAAP) including Business Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, Cost Concept, Dual Aspect, Revenue Recognition, Matching, Full Disclosure, Consistency, Conservatism, Materiality, and Objectivity.
Analyze the effect of business transactions on assets, liabilities, capital, revenue, and expenses using the accounting equation approach.
Prepare source documents and vouchers for various trade discounts, freight, cartage, and GST scenarios.
Record business transactions in Books of Original Entry including Journal (with special entries), Cash Book (Simple, Bank column, Petty cashbook) and Special Purpose Books.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-3: Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship
outcomes: Understand the core concepts of Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship.
Apply key ideas from Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship to solve curriculum-aligned problems.
Analyze relationships between major concepts in Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship.

learning_outcomes.topic: Foundations of Accounting and Theoretical Framework
outcomes: Explain the concept, objectives, and limitations of accounting along with its role in business.
Record accounting transactions accurately using the double entry system including journals, cash books, ledgers, and depreciation calculations.

learning_outcomes.topic: Analysis of Financial Statements and Cash Flow
outcomes: Interpret financial data through ratio analysis and prepare a statement of cash flows.
Operate basic functions of accounting software to record transactions and generate financial statements.

learning_outcomes.topic: Cash Flow Statement Preparation and Analysis
outcomes: Construct a Cash Flow Statement by classifying inflows and outflows from operating, investing, and financing activities.
Identify key differences between manual and computerized accounting systems regarding efficiency and accuracy.
Explain the role of hardware and software requirements for implementing a successful computerized accounting solution.
Analyze the impact of automation on data entry, processing speed, and error reduction in financial record-keeping.

projects.title: Project Work: Collection of Source Documents and Recording
projects.details: Collection of source documents, preparation of vouchers, recording of transactions with the help of vouchers.

projects.title: Project Work: Bank Reconciliation Statement
projects.details: Preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement with the given cash book and the pass book with twenty to twenty-five transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Comprehensive Sole Proprietorship Business
projects.details: Comprehensive project of any sole proprietorship business. This may start with journal entries and their ledgering, preparation of Trial balance, Trading and Profit and Loss Account and Balance Sheet. Expenses, incomes and profit (loss), assets and liabilities are to be depicted using pie chart / bar diagram. May include simple GST related transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Financial Statement Analysis
projects.details: One specific project based on financial statement analysis of a company covering any two aspects from the following: Comparative and common size financial statements, Accounting Ratios, Segment Reports, Cash Flow Statements.

practicals.title: Practical Work: Computerised Accounting System
practicals.details: Steps in installation of CAS, codification and Hierarchy of account heads, creation of accounts; Data Entry, validation and verification; Adjusting entries, preparation of balance sheet, profit and loss account with closing entries and opening entries.

dependency_graph.topic: Foundations of Accounting and Theoretical Framework (Class XII)

lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Theory Questions on Concepts and Assumptions

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Source documents preparation and transaction recording exercises
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Journal entries, Cash Book, Special Purpose Books

lesson_planning_intelligence.unit_name: Unit-3: Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Preparation of Trading and P&L Account, Balance Sheet

lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Complex problem solving on Partnership Firms

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Company accounts and share capital issues
Software installation and data entry practice
Full cycle accounting using software (Installation, Codification, Data Entry)
